name: Greater Lakes Transportation
districts_or_customers: Medicaid/NEMT clients in Oakland County, MI; no school district contracts identified
recent_news: No M&A or growth news identified; listed in NPI registry as non-emergency medical transport van provider
fit_notes: Poor fit for Summit tuck-in: micro NEMT van operator (1-5 employees), no school bus or SPED district contracts identified. Sub-scale and outside Summit's school-transportation focus.

name: Renali Transport
districts_or_customers: No school district contracts identified; small NEMT operator in metro Detroit
fit_notes: Poor fit for Summit tuck-in: micro Michigan LLC (~5 employees) incorporated 2010, appears to be NEMT/medical transport not school bus. Sub-scale with no school-district customer base identified.

name: Medistar Inc
principals: Mark Binder (former VP Operations); Smolov (former CFO)
service_types: Formerly non-emergency medical transportation (NEMT); not a school bus contractor
recent_news: Filed bankruptcy June 23, 2015; subject of NATAL v. MEDISTAR, INC. federal employment classification litigation (221 F.Supp.3d 999, 2016)
fit_notes: NOT a viable target: defunct NEMT operator that filed bankruptcy in 2015. Never a school bus contractor. Remove from pipeline.

name: Discover Ride
service_types: Non-Emergency Medical Transportation (NEMT) for individuals with mobility challenges and some special-needs children; not school district pupil transport
districts_or_customers: Individual NEMT riders across Twin Cities metro (Minneapolis/St. Paul); no school district contracts identified
fit_notes: Poor fit for Summit tuck-in: small NEMT operator focused on individual medical rides, not school-district contracted pupil transportation. Sub-scale (~4 employees) and outside Summit's core school-bus segment, though some special-needs child transport referenced.

name: Big Star Transit LLC
principals: Tanya Biggers (CEO/Co-founder); Larry Biggers Sr. (COO/Co-founder); Larry Biggers Jr. (Chief Revenue Officer)
fleet_size_est: network of independent owner-operators (no centralized fleet)
employees_est: ~4 corporate (plus network of contracted owner-operator drivers)
service_types: NEMT and paratransit via owner-operator network model; not traditional school bus contractor
districts_or_customers: Paratransit and NEMT riders in DFW area; no public school district pupil-transport contracts identified
recent_news: None material; founders have 20+ years industry background; operates as network/MBE-aggregator model rather than fleet operator
fit_notes: Poor fit for Summit tuck-in: NEMT/paratransit aggregator using owner-operator network rather than owned bus fleet; family-led (Biggers) with next-gen already in CRO role (low succession signal). Different business model from Summit's school-bus contracting platform.
